2026-03-06.content:
Just like setting a global rule in Cursor, I feel that my life also needs a global rule. What principles should I follow? What priorities should guide my decisions and actions?  AI systems sometimes drift when executing tasks, and I found that I can drift as well, especially when facing a constantly changing world, many temporary ideas, and various kinds of temptations.

Priorities:
1. Physical exercise and health
2. Spending uninterrupted and protected time with God every day
3. Marriage: involving my wife and spending time with her
4. Leading and helping my children in matters of faith and character
5. Within limited time, staying as focused as possible in my work
6. Hobbies: AI programming, woodworking, and fishing

I need to review these priorities regularly (at least once a month) and adjust them when necessary

2026-03-07.content:
These days we are about to enter the pollen season in March, and my body has already begun to show some allergy symptoms. My eyes are extremely itchy, I have started coughing, my body feels sore, and my throat is uncomfortable. When I think back to last year’s experience, from early March to around early May, it was almost two months of feeling very miserable. Even now, when I think about it, I still feel a bit uneasy.

One big challenge for me is this: when my body does not feel well, my mood tends to become low.

When my mood is low, my attitude toward my wife and children can sometimes become cold, and at times I may appear negative.

This also affects my own spiritual state.

Hebrews 4:1 says: “Therefore, since the promise of entering his rest still stands, let us be careful that none of you be found to have fallen short of it.” (NIV)

The word “fear” or “reverence” appears frequently in this book. It is not a kind of anxious worry about losing salvation, but rather a reverence toward God that comes from knowing one’s identity in Him.

It is not only about worrying that I might lose salvation, but also about being concerned that someone in the community might fall short. From myself, this concern extends outward to care for other Christians around me.

Verse 10 of chapter 4 says: “for anyone who enters God’s rest also rests from their works, just as God did from his.” I do not fully understand what it means to “rest from one’s works.” What are “one’s works”? These “works” do not refer to everyday work or life activities. To rest from one’s works means no longer relying on oneself, no longer depending on behavior to earn salvation, no longer depending on behavior to gain the approval of others, and no longer depending on behavior to obtain a spiritual identity.

Resting from one’s works does not mean doing nothing. On the contrary, verse 11 says: “Let us, therefore, make every effort to enter that rest, so that no one will perish by following their example of disobedience.” This seems like a very unusual state: making every effort to enter rest.

Hebrews 4:7 again mentions the word “Today,” which touches me once more: “Today, if you hear his voice, do not harden your hearts.” This is a reminder to me that today, when I hear God’s voice and read His word, I should not harden my heart.

2026-03-11.content:
So do not throw away your confidence; it will bring you a greater reward.
Hebrews 10:35

"And my righteous one will live by faith; and if he shrinks back, I will not be pleased with him"
Hebrews 10:38

Lord Jesus Christ, you offered your body once for all. According to the will of the Father, through the body you offered once for all, I have been made holy.

God the Father no longer remembers my sins and transgressions. I thank and praise you, for through your blood I can enter the most holy place with confidence.

Please help me not to throw away my courageous heart. In every situation, help me to stand firm and persevere.

Make me willing to bear witness to you and glorify you in my daily life and on social media. Keep me from shrinking back, for shrinking back does not please you.

In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, I pray, Amen.

Thanksgiving: 
1. Thank God for His protection. It seems that this year my pollen allergy is not as severe as last year. My body is gradually adapting and I can still spend some time outdoors each day.
2. Thank God for protecting Linda. She arrived safely in Munich to serve there. May the Lord keep her safe, be with her, and move her heart.
3. The new product I made has now had six downloads and one user has paid for it. This is an encouragement to me. May it be encouragement rather than stirring up greed in my heart.
